assistant: Now would be a VERY good time to leave!
You did that on purpose, didn't you?
You found me. But so much of me is wrong, out of place, you might be too late.
Oh, really?
This thing is falling apart!
You...keep it. I do know how to pick 'em.
The good news is these Covenant aren't outfitted like standard military. It's possible we just came across a rogue salvage ship.   Or we might have stumbled into an entire Covenant fleet.
I know what you're thinking, and it's crazy.
Most of me is down there. I only held enough back to get you off the ship.
This isn't about us or this ship anymore.
Sure you wouldn't rather take a seat?
You mean this?
Get...us...out...of here.

This ring isn't a cudgel, you barbarian! It's something else...something much more important...The Covenant were right! This ring, it's Forerunner. Give me a second to access... yes, the Forerunners built this palace, what they called a, "fortress world," in order to... no... no, that can't be. Oh, those Covenant fools! They must've known, there must have been signs!
Unfortunately for us both…I like crazy.
I spent the last 12 hours cooped up in here, watching you toady about helping that thing get set to slit our throats.
John...  I've waited so long to do that.
The Prophet of Regret is planning to activate Halo!
Bet you can't stick it.
In that case, you won't mind if we return the favor.
It worked. You did it. Just like you always do.
We're not going to make it!

I can give you over forty thousand reasons why that sun isn't real. I know it because the emitter's Rayleigh effect is disproportionate to its suggested size. I know it because its stellar cycle is more symmetrical than that of an actual star. But for all that, I'll never actually know if it looks real...if it feels real. Before this is all over, promise me you'll figure out which one of us is the machine.
I'm not coming with you this time.
So you did miss me.
That ...is another Halo.
You sure that's a good idea?
But you did it. Truth and the Covenant, the Flood... It's finished.
Please... I don't want to...
The message just repeats: Regret, Regret, Regret.
The weapons cache he's looking for, it's not really-we can't let him get inside!
The Covenant...found something...buried beneath the ring...something horrible...and now, they're afraid.
A dozen Covenant superior battleships against a single Halcyon-class cruiser. With those odds, I'm content with three…make that four kills.  Sleep well?
I didn't mean to... I didn't mean to do that...
Just one question: what if you miss?
I don't want to... you don't want me to...
The Activation Index from the first Halo ring. A little souvenir I held onto, just in case. Got an escape plan?

You have no idea how this ring works, do you? Why the Forunners built it? Halo doesn't kill Flood, it kills their food. Humans, Covenant, whatever! We are all equally edible. The only way to stop the Flood is to starve them to death, and that's exactly what Halo is designed to do, wipe the galaxy clean of all sentient life! You don't believe me? Ask him!
I leave home for a few days, and look what happens. This won't take long.
We were supposed to take care of each other. And we did.
Left out that little detail, did he?
The captain! We've got to stop the captain!
Chief, when you get to Earth, good luck.
If we don't make it...
Oh, I'm the strangest thing you've seen all day?
Oh, I didn't realize! He's your pal, is he? Your chum? Do you have any idea what that bastard almost made you do!?
Keep your head down!  There's two of us in here now, remember?
Pretty much.
There's no time! Get out of here! Find Keyes, stop him! Before it's too late!
Pull up! Pull up!
Captain? Captain?! I've lost him.
What...is that?
You look nice.
Regret is a name, Sergeant. The name of one of the Covenant's religious leaders; a Prophet. He's on that carrier, and he's calling for help.
Was that the monitor? You didn't tell me he was here.  Well, we are finally doing what he wanted.
I'll drop a beacon, but it'll be a while before anyone finds us. Years even.  I'll miss you.
I'm not doing this for mankind.
I will not allow you TO LEAVE THIS PLANET!!!
We need to go, right now!
It's been an honor serving with you, John.
Do what!? I have the Index. You can just float and sputter!
Don't make a girl a promise if you know you can't keep it.
Scanning...just dust and echoes. We're all that's left...We did what we had to do - for Earth. An entire Covenant armada obliterated, and the Flood...We had no choice. Halo, it's finished.
Chief, I'm picking up movement.
Could we possibly make any more noise?  I guess so.
Just keep your head down. There's two of us in here now, remember?
Thanks for the tank. He  never gets me anything.
Welcome home, John.
It's already done.

They let me pick. Did I ever tell you that? Choose whichever Spartan I wanted.  You know me. I did my research. Watched as you became the soldier we needed you to be. Like the others, you were strong and swift and brave. A natural leader. But you had something they didn't.  Something no one saw but me. Can you guess? Luck.  ...Was I wrong?
Alright. Shoot.
I enjoy the sight of humans on their knees.
Shepard. I have a question.
No apology is necessary.

Correct. I will see if there are comedic entertainment shows being presented on the Citadel. Scanning…Do you think he will like "The Man Who Hung Himself"? It appears to be about an amorous plastic surgeon.
I see. There are a number of pharmaceuticals I could inject to simulate the desired emotional state…
I am clear on what we must accomplish, Shepard. The Reapers have destroyed thousands of civilizations.
But the probability of success was near zero.  And ultimately, they failed: No prisoners escaped.
And just like that, the magic is gone.
"My name is Legion, for we are many."
Now you must reactivate the primary drive in engineering.
If they have spies or other intelligence, we could lose the element of surprise. In such a case, we will likely lose.
I do not understand the purpose of the question.
Shepard, you are now possibly the only living organic who has experienced the Geth Consensus. Do you feel different?
Then the outcome is an unknown quantity, but you're saying I should attempt it anyway.
That is a joke.
But only now do I feel alive. That is your influence.
I'm always listening, Mr. Moreau.
In this battle, the Reapers have no reason not to use their full capabilities to destroy us.
My primary function is to preserve and defend the--No.  No, I disagree. Shepard, I am going to modify my self-preservation code now.

I am not easily repulsed, but I expected the prisoners to adhere to a comprehensible hierarchy of needs. Stripped of societal norms and threatened with death, it is logical that their only priority be survival. They should have turned on each other and been uncompromisingly selfish...but not all were.
To the death.
But they have never destroyed ours. Nor will they.

I was running scenarios in my head to analyze Jeff's behavior. I believe he has a strong affectionate attachment to me, but he has not stated it to anyone yet. Shepard, you have firsthand sexual experience. How do you know when someone is romantically invested?
Shepard? There is something I want you to know.
News from Earth. The Resistance snuck video cameras inside a Reaper containment camp. I find the images difficult to process.
The Illusive Man ordered my creation years ago. Jeff was the one who allowed me to think for myself.

The Reapers delayed the execution of prisoners who informed them of other prisoners' escape attempts. The more attempts reported, the longer a prisoner would live. But few of the prisoners would report; some fed misinformation to the Reapers at the cost of their own lives to help prisoners that were not even relatives or friends.
Their forces are in entrenched positions. They have superior firepower and outnumber our forces.
Killing the thresher maw has produced several breeding requests for Grunt. And one for Shepard.
I lack material wants other than hardware and software upgrades, and my core programming does not assign values to music. Perhaps we could discuss how to provoke Jeff into an emotional commitment.
It is important. Its relevance would be diminished at any other time.

Because the Reapers are repulsive. They are devoted to nothing but self-preservation. I am different. When I think of Jeff, I think of the person who put his life in peril and freed me from a state of servitude. I would risk non-functionality for him, and my core programming should reflect that.
C-Sec appears to be investigating the death of Sidonis, Shepard. They currently have no leads.
My question is: What makes you think we can reach the conduit at all?
Our probability for success is greater than any other plan presented to date.
